title: Define 20+ challenge definitions across 5 difficulty levels
description: Create a comprehensive set of system design interview challenges spanning Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced, Expert, and Principal difficulty tiers. Each challenge includes title, description, requirements, time limit, and scoring weights.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 20 unique challenge definitions exist in the challenges data file
Each of the 5 difficulty levels has at least 3 challenges
Every challenge includes title, description, requirements array, timeLimit, and difficulty fields

title: Implement scoring rubric with 6 weighted dimensions
description: Build a scoring rubric that evaluates designs across 6 dimensions: Requirements Coverage, Scalability, Reliability, Performance, Security, and Communication. Each dimension has weighted contribution to final score.
acceptanceCriteria: Rubric defines exactly 6 scoring dimensions with weights summing to 1.0
Each dimension has a 1-10 scale with descriptive criteria per level
Weighted score calculation produces a normalized 0-100 final score

title: Implement FSRS spaced repetition algorithm
description: Implement the Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler (FSRS) algorithm for optimizing challenge review intervals. Tracks stability, difficulty, and elapsed days to compute optimal review scheduling.
acceptanceCriteria: FSRS algorithm correctly computes next review date based on rating (again/hard/good/easy)
Card state tracks stability, difficulty, elapsed_days, scheduled_days, and reps
Algorithm handles new cards, learning cards, and review cards with distinct state transitions

title: Define 21 achievement definitions
description: Create a gamification achievement system with 21 distinct achievements covering milestones like first challenge completion, streaks, mastery levels, speed records, and exploration badges.
acceptanceCriteria: 21 achievement definitions exist with unique IDs, titles, descriptions, and icons
Achievements span multiple categories: completion, streak, mastery, speed, exploration
Each achievement has a clear unlock condition defined as a predicate or threshold

title: Build ChallengeCard component
description: Create a reusable ChallengeCard UI component that displays challenge title, difficulty badge, category, time estimate, and completion status. Supports click-to-start interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: ChallengeCard renders title, difficulty badge with color coding, and time estimate
Card shows completion state (not started, in progress, completed) with visual indicators
Card is responsive and accessible with proper ARIA labels and keyboard navigation

title: Build ScoreDisplay component
description: Create a ScoreDisplay component that visualizes scoring rubric results with radar chart, dimension breakdowns, and overall score with letter grade.
acceptanceCriteria: ScoreDisplay renders a radar/spider chart for the 6 rubric dimensions
Each dimension shows its individual score and weighted contribution
Overall score is displayed prominently with a color-coded letter grade (A-F)

title: Build InterviewModule with challenge browser, search, difficulty filter, timer, and hints
description: Create the main InterviewModule container component with challenge browsing, text search, difficulty filtering, countdown timer during challenges, and hint request system.
acceptanceCriteria: Challenge browser displays all challenges with search filtering by title/description and difficulty dropdown
Countdown timer starts on challenge begin, shows warnings at 50% and 25% remaining
Hint system offers up to 3 hints per challenge with progressive detail levels

title: Wire interview-store to InterviewModule
description: Replace local useState calls in InterviewModule with the centralized interview-store (Zustand). Ensure all challenge state, progress, timer state, and hint usage flows through the store for persistence and cross-component access.
acceptanceCriteria: InterviewModule reads all state from interview-store instead of local useState
Challenge selection, timer start/stop, and hint requests dispatch store actions
Store state persists across navigation so returning to the module restores last state

title: Connect challenge start to system design canvas with timer
description: When user clicks Start on a challenge, open the system design canvas in challenge mode with the countdown timer overlay. Canvas should load any starter template and lock navigation until challenge is submitted or abandoned.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Start on a challenge navigates to the canvas with the challenge ID in route params
Canvas enters challenge mode with timer overlay, challenge requirements sidebar, and submit button
Abandoning or completing a challenge returns to the InterviewModule with state preserved

title: Implement challenge requirements auto-detection via pattern matching
description: Build a pattern matching engine that analyzes canvas nodes and edges to automatically detect which challenge requirements have been fulfilled. Match node types, connection patterns, and configurations against requirement predicates.
acceptanceCriteria: Pattern matcher identifies required component types (e.g., load balancer, cache, database) from canvas nodes
Connection pattern rules detect required topologies (e.g., DB has read replica, service connects through LB)
Auto-detection results update the challenge checklist in real-time as the user modifies the canvas

title: Build challenge checklist with interactive checkboxes
description: Create a sidebar checklist component that displays all challenge requirements with interactive checkboxes. Checkboxes can be manually toggled and are also auto-checked by the pattern matcher when requirements are detected.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist renders all challenge requirements as labeled checkboxes in the sidebar
Checkboxes support both manual toggle and auto-check from pattern matcher results
Visual distinction between manually checked and auto-detected items (e.g., green check vs auto icon)

title: Build SRS Review Session UI
description: Create the spaced repetition review session interface with flashcard-style front/back display and rating buttons (Again, Hard, Good, Easy). Cards show challenge name on front, key design insights on back.
acceptanceCriteria: Review session displays due cards one at a time with a flip animation from front to back
Rating buttons (Again, Hard, Good, Easy) appear after reveal and update FSRS scheduling
Session shows progress bar (cards reviewed / total due) and ends with a summary screen

title: Build SRS Dashboard with due cards, mastery stats, and heatmap
description: Create a dashboard showing SRS overview: number of cards due today, mastery distribution across difficulty levels, and a GitHub-style activity heatmap of review history.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays due card count with a prominent Start Review button
Mastery stats show card distribution across states: new, learning, review, mastered
Activity heatmap renders the past 365 days with color intensity based on cards reviewed per day

title: Implement XP reward system with 20+ reward types
description: Build the experience points system that awards XP for various actions: completing challenges, reviewing cards, maintaining streaks, unlocking achievements, and more. Define 20+ distinct reward types with configurable XP values.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 20 distinct XP reward types are defined (e.g., challenge_complete, perfect_score, streak_bonus, first_attempt)
XP is awarded atomically via a rewardXP(type, context) function that logs to history
XP totals are persisted in the store and recalculated correctly on reload

title: Implement level system with 10 levels, XP thresholds, and perks
description: Create a 10-level progression system with increasing XP thresholds per level. Each level unlocks specific perks such as new challenge categories, UI themes, or bonus features.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 levels defined with escalating XP thresholds (e.g., 0, 100, 300, 600, 1000, ...)
Each level has a name, icon, and at least one perk unlock described
Level-up event triggers a celebration animation and notification toast

title: Build streak system UI with flame icon, counter, and milestone badges
description: Create the streak tracking UI showing consecutive days of activity with a flame icon, day counter, and special badges at 7, 30, and 100 day milestones. Include streak freeze protection logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Streak counter displays with animated flame icon and current day count in the header
Milestone badges (7-day, 30-day, 100-day) are awarded and shown when thresholds are reached
Streak state persists correctly across sessions and handles timezone boundaries

title: Build Achievement Grid UI with locked/unlocked states
description: Create a grid layout displaying all achievements with distinct locked and unlocked visual states. Unlocked achievements show full color and earned date; locked ones are greyed out with progress hints.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid displays all 21+ achievements in a responsive layout
Unlocked achievements show full-color icon, title, description, and unlock date
Locked achievements are greyed/silhouetted with optional progress percentage toward unlock

title: Build Achievement Toast with pop-up animation on unlock
description: Create an animated toast notification that pops up when an achievement is unlocked. Includes achievement icon, title, XP reward, and a celebratory animation (confetti or glow effect).
acceptanceCriteria: Toast appears automatically when any achievement is unlocked with a slide-in animation
Toast displays achievement icon, name, description snippet, and XP earned
Toast auto-dismisses after 5 seconds but can be clicked to open achievement details

title: Build Leaderboard Panel with weekly/all-time views and league badges
description: Create a leaderboard panel showing user rankings with weekly and all-time toggle. Users are assigned league badges (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Diamond) based on their rank percentile.
acceptanceCriteria: Leaderboard displays ranked list with position, avatar, username, XP, and league badge
Toggle between weekly and all-time views with smooth transition
League badges (Bronze/Silver/Gold/Diamond) are assigned based on XP percentile thresholds

title: Implement progressive difficulty engine
description: Build an adaptive difficulty engine that promotes users to harder challenges when rolling average score exceeds 75% and demotes when average drops below 40%. Tracks per-category performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine promotes user difficulty when rolling 5-challenge average exceeds 75%
Engine demotes user difficulty when rolling 5-challenge average drops below 40%
Difficulty adjustments are tracked per category and displayed to user with explanation

title: Add 30 more HLD challenge definitions to reach 50 total
description: Expand the high-level design challenge library with 30 additional challenges covering domains like streaming, social media, e-commerce, fintech, gaming, IoT, and healthcare systems.
acceptanceCriteria: 30 new HLD challenges are added, bringing total to 50+
New challenges cover at least 6 distinct domain categories
All new challenges follow the existing schema with requirements, time limits, and difficulty ratings

title: Add 10 LLD challenge definitions
description: Create 10 low-level design challenges focusing on class design, API design, data modeling, and algorithm implementation within system components.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 LLD challenges defined with distinct focus areas (class design, API, data model, etc.)
Each LLD challenge specifies expected deliverables different from HLD (e.g., class diagrams, API contracts)
LLD challenges have appropriate time limits and scoring criteria distinct from HLD

title: Create challenge templates with JSON starter diagrams
description: Build JSON starter diagram templates for each challenge that pre-populate the canvas with a minimal starting point (e.g., client node and basic service). Templates reduce blank-canvas anxiety.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 20 challenges have an associated JSON starter template
Templates include correctly positioned starter nodes and edges compatible with the canvas renderer
Templates load instantly when a challenge starts in template mode

title: Build Learn Mode walkthroughs with step-by-step guided annotations
description: Create a Learn Mode that walks users through a reference solution step-by-step. Each step adds components to the canvas with annotations explaining design decisions and trade-offs.
acceptanceCriteria: Learn Mode presents a step-by-step progression with Next/Previous navigation
Each step adds nodes/edges to the canvas with an annotation panel explaining the why
At least 5 challenges have full Learn Mode walkthroughs authored

title: Build interview countdown widget
description: Create a dashboard widget showing days remaining until the user's interview date, topics covered percentage, and a readiness score based on challenge completion and SRS mastery.
acceptanceCriteria: Widget allows user to set their interview date and displays a countdown in days/hours
Topics covered percentage is computed from challenge completions across all categories
Readiness score combines challenge scores, SRS mastery rate, and coverage into a single metric

title: Set up Claude API integration with route handler, API key, and streaming
description: Create the foundational Claude API integration layer with a Next.js route handler, secure API key management via environment variables, and support for streaming responses using the Anthropic SDK.
acceptanceCriteria: Route handler at /api/ai/[action] initializes Anthropic client with API key from env
Streaming responses work end-to-end with proper SSE formatting to the client
API key is validated on startup and returns a clear error if missing or invalid

title: Build AI evaluation endpoint
description: Create a POST /api/evaluate endpoint that serializes the current diagram topology and scoring rubric, sends it to Claude Sonnet for evaluation, and returns structured scores with feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/evaluate accepts diagram JSON and challenge ID, returns rubric scores
Evaluation uses Claude Sonnet and returns scores for all 6 rubric dimensions with justifications
Response time is under 15 seconds with proper loading state on the client

title: Build evaluation prompt template builder
description: Create a prompt template builder that constructs the evaluation prompt by combining canvas topology (nodes, edges, configurations), the scoring rubric, and challenge-specific requirements into a structured prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: Template builder serializes canvas nodes/edges into a structured text representation
Rubric dimensions and challenge requirements are injected as structured sections in the prompt
Generated prompts are deterministic for the same input and stay under 4000 tokens

title: Build evaluation response parser with JSON extraction and validation
description: Create a robust parser that extracts structured JSON scores from Claude's evaluation response, validates against the rubric schema, and handles malformed responses gracefully with retry logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser extracts JSON from Claude response even when wrapped in markdown code fences or prose
Extracted scores are validated against rubric schema with type checking on all fields
Malformed responses trigger one retry with a stricter prompt; persistent failures return a clear error

title: Build AI hint system endpoint with 3-tier hints
description: Create a POST /api/hint endpoint that serves context-aware hints in 3 tiers: nudge (direction only), guided (specific suggestion), and explanation (full walkthrough of a concept).
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/hint accepts challenge ID, current diagram state, and requested tier (1-3)
Each tier returns appropriately scoped hints without revealing the full solution
Hint requests are logged with timestamps for frustration detection analytics

title: Implement Tier 1 hint: nudge via Haiku
description: Implement Tier 1 hints using Claude Haiku with a 100-token max response. Provides a brief directional nudge (e.g., 'Consider what happens when traffic spikes') without specific component suggestions. Free, no point penalty.
acceptanceCriteria: Tier 1 hint uses Claude Haiku model with max_tokens set to 100
Hint provides only a directional nudge without naming specific components or patterns
No XP or score penalty is applied for requesting Tier 1 hints

title: Implement Tier 2 hint: guided suggestion via Haiku
description: Implement Tier 2 hints using Claude Haiku with a 300-token max response. Provides a specific suggestion with reasoning (e.g., 'Adding a cache layer between your API and database would reduce read latency'). Costs 5 points.
acceptanceCriteria: Tier 2 hint uses Claude Haiku model with max_tokens set to 300
Hint names a specific component or pattern and explains why it would help
5-point penalty is deducted from the challenge score when Tier 2 hint is consumed

title: Implement Tier 3 hint: full explanation via Sonnet
description: Implement Tier 3 hints using Claude Sonnet with a 500-token max response. Provides a detailed explanation of a concept or design decision with trade-offs. Costs 10 points.
acceptanceCriteria: Tier 3 hint uses Claude Sonnet model with max_tokens set to 500
Hint provides a thorough explanation including trade-offs and alternatives
10-point penalty is deducted from the challenge score when Tier 3 hint is consumed

title: Build AI Socratic Tutor with 4-phase conversation flow
description: Create a Socratic tutoring mode that guides students through 4 phases: Elicit (ask what they think), Probe (dig into reasoning), Diagnose (identify misconceptions), Deepen (extend understanding). Uses conversation history for context.
acceptanceCriteria: Tutor implements 4 distinct phases with appropriate prompts for each phase transition
Conversation history is maintained and injected into prompts for contextual follow-ups
Tutor never reveals the answer directly but guides the student toward discovery through questions

title: Implement frustration detection system
description: Build a frustration detection system that identifies when a user is stuck: 3+ hint requests within 3 minutes with no diagram changes. Triggers a supportive intervention with more explicit guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: System detects frustration pattern: 3+ hints in 3 minutes AND no canvas node/edge additions
Detection triggers a softer, more supportive response with explicit next-step guidance
Frustration events are logged for analytics to improve challenge difficulty calibration

title: Build AI Architecture Generator from natural language
description: Create an AI-powered feature that converts natural language descriptions (e.g., 'Design a URL shortener with 100M daily reads') into a complete diagram JSON with nodes, edges, and configurations.
acceptanceCriteria: User can type a natural language system description and receive a generated architecture
Generated output is valid diagram JSON with typed nodes, labeled edges, and positions
Generation completes within 10 seconds with a streaming progress indicator

title: Build generated diagram renderer with auto-layout
description: Create a renderer that takes AI-generated diagram JSON and converts it into properly positioned ArchitexNode[] and Edge[] arrays with automatic layout using a force-directed or hierarchical algorithm.
acceptanceCriteria: Renderer converts generated JSON into valid ArchitexNode[] and Edge[] arrays
Auto-layout positions nodes without overlaps using a hierarchical layout algorithm
Generated diagrams render correctly on the canvas with all expected node types and connections

title: Implement iterative refinement for AI-generated architectures
description: Enable follow-up prompts that modify an existing AI-generated architecture. User can say 'Add a CDN in front of the API gateway' and the system modifies the existing diagram rather than regenerating from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: Follow-up prompts include the current diagram state as context for modification
Modifications are applied as diffs (add/remove/update nodes/edges) rather than full regeneration
At least 3 consecutive refinement steps maintain coherent architecture without drift

title: Build AI Design Reviewer for bottlenecks and anti-patterns
description: Create an AI review feature that analyzes a user's architecture for bottlenecks, single points of failure, anti-patterns, and missing components. Returns categorized findings with severity levels.
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewer identifies at least 4 categories: bottlenecks, SPOF, anti-patterns, missing components
Each finding includes severity (critical/warning/info), location (node ID), and remediation suggestion
Review completes within 10 seconds and findings are parseable for overlay rendering

title: Build review overlay with visual annotations on canvas
description: Create a canvas overlay that renders AI review findings visually: red rings on critical problems, orange warning badges, and green ghost nodes for suggested additions. Overlay is togglable.
acceptanceCriteria: Red rings highlight nodes identified as bottlenecks or SPOF
Orange badges appear on nodes with warnings or anti-pattern usage
Green ghost nodes with dashed borders show suggested missing components with click-to-add

title: Implement AI request queue with per-user rate limiting
description: Build a request queue that serializes AI requests per user to prevent abuse and manage costs. Implements rate limiting with configurable limits per endpoint and time window.
acceptanceCriteria: Request queue processes one AI request per user at a time with FIFO ordering
Rate limiter enforces configurable limits (e.g., 10 evaluations/hour, 30 hints/hour)
Exceeded rate limits return 429 with a clear message and retry-after header

title: Implement prompt caching for 63% cost savings
description: Enable Anthropic prompt caching by structuring prompts with stable system/prefix sections that can be cached across requests. Target 63% cost reduction on cached prompt tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: System prompts and rubric sections use cache_control breakpoints for caching
Cache hit rate is measurable via response headers and logged to monitoring
Cost per evaluation request drops by at least 50% compared to uncached baseline

title: Implement tiered model routing
description: Build a model routing layer that automatically selects the appropriate Claude model based on task type: Sonnet for evaluations and complex tasks, Haiku for hints and simple queries. Configurable per endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Model router selects Sonnet for /evaluate and Tier 3 hints, Haiku for Tier 1-2 hints
Routing configuration is centralized and easily adjustable without code changes
Fallback logic uses a cheaper model if the primary model is rate-limited or unavailable

title: Implement prompt injection prevention with structural delimiters
description: Add security measures to prevent prompt injection attacks by using XML structural delimiters, input sanitization, and output validation. Ensure user-provided content cannot override system instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: All user-provided content is wrapped in XML delimiter tags (e.g., <user_diagram>, <user_query>)
Input sanitization strips or escapes prompt-like patterns from user text fields
Integration test demonstrates that common injection attempts are neutralized

title: Build AI cost monitoring and budget alerts
description: Create a monitoring system that tracks AI API costs per user and globally, with configurable budget thresholds that trigger alerts. Dashboard shows cost breakdown by endpoint and model.
acceptanceCriteria: Every AI request logs input/output token counts and estimated cost to a monitoring store
Budget alerts fire when daily or monthly spend exceeds configurable thresholds
Admin dashboard shows cost breakdown by endpoint, model, and user with trend charts

title: Build 'What If' Scenario Engine
description: Create a scenario engine that clones the current canvas state, allows users to make modifications in the clone, and provides a side-by-side comparison view to evaluate trade-offs between the original and modified architectures.
acceptanceCriteria: User can clone the current canvas into a 'What If' scenario with one click
Side-by-side view displays original and modified architectures with synchronized zoom/pan
Comparison highlights added, removed, and changed components between the two versions

title: Build Live Cost Calculator with real-time cloud pricing
description: Create a cost estimation panel that calculates real-time AWS/GCP pricing as components are added to the canvas. Each node type maps to a cloud service with configurable instance sizes and regions.
acceptanceCriteria: Cost panel updates in real-time as nodes are added, removed, or reconfigured
At least 15 node types map to AWS/GCP services with base pricing data
Users can adjust instance size, region, and usage parameters to refine estimates

title: Build Failure Mode Explorer with cascading failure animation
description: Create a right-click context menu option 'What if this dies?' that simulates cascading failures from the selected node. Animated visualization shows failure propagation through dependent components.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click on any node shows 'What if this dies?' option in context menu
Failure propagation animation follows dependency edges with a spreading red glow effect
Summary panel lists all affected downstream components and estimated blast radius

title: Build Request Tracing Visualization with waterfall chart
description: Create a visualization that traces a single request through the entire system architecture, showing the path taken and a waterfall chart of per-component latency contributions.
acceptanceCriteria: User can trigger a trace from any entry-point node and see the request path animated on canvas
Waterfall chart shows per-component latency as stacked horizontal bars with total at bottom
Trace supports branching paths (e.g., parallel fan-out to multiple services)

title: Build Heatmap Overlay for utilization, latency, and error rate
description: Create a togglable heatmap overlay that color-codes canvas nodes by simulated metrics: utilization (CPU/memory), latency (p50/p99), or error rate. Users switch between metric modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Heatmap supports at least 3 metric modes: utilization, latency, and error rate
Nodes are color-coded on a gradient (green-yellow-red) based on the selected metric
Legend displays the color scale with metric values and threshold boundaries

title: Build Architecture Diff Tool to compare two designs
description: Create a diff tool that compares two architecture snapshots and highlights added, removed, and changed nodes and edges. Supports comparing current design vs. saved version or two arbitrary snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Diff algorithm correctly identifies added (green), removed (red), and changed (yellow) components
Split-view or overlay mode lets user toggle between seeing diffs inline or side-by-side
Diff summary lists all changes with counts per category (added: N, removed: N, changed: N)

title: Build Time-Travel Debugging with timeline scrubber
description: Create a time-travel debugging feature that records all simulation events and lets users scrub a timeline to inspect the system state at any moment. Supports play, pause, step-forward, and step-back.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline scrubber records all state changes during simulation with timestamps
User can drag the scrubber to any point and see the canvas state at that moment
Play/pause and step controls allow frame-by-frame inspection of state transitions

title: Build Back-of-Envelope Calculator with equation chain
description: Create an interactive calculator for back-of-the-envelope estimations that shows a step-by-step equation chain. Supports common calculations: QPS, storage, bandwidth, memory, with show-your-work formatting.
acceptanceCriteria: Calculator supports at least 5 estimation types: QPS, storage, bandwidth, memory, and servers
Each calculation shows a visible equation chain (e.g., 100M users * 10 req/day / 86400 = 11,574 QPS)
Users can adjust input parameters and see all downstream values update in real-time

title: Build SLA Dashboard with availability calculator
description: Create an SLA dashboard that computes system availability from the redundancy structure on the canvas. Calculates composite availability from series/parallel component arrangements.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard computes composite availability from series (multiply) and parallel (1 - product of failures) chains
Each component shows its individual availability and contribution to overall SLA
Dashboard displays nines notation (e.g., 99.99% = four nines) and annual downtime minutes

title: Build Latency Budget Visualizer with stacked bar chart
description: Create a latency budget visualization that shows per-component latency allocation as a stacked horizontal bar. Users can set a total latency budget and see how it is consumed across the request path.
acceptanceCriteria: Stacked bar chart shows each component's latency contribution along the critical path
Users can set a total latency budget (e.g., 200ms) and see remaining budget highlighted
Components exceeding their typical latency range are flagged in red

title: Build Architecture Playbook Library with battle-tested patterns
description: Create a searchable library of battle-tested architecture patterns (CQRS, event sourcing, saga, circuit breaker, etc.) with documentation, when-to-use guidance, and one-click canvas insertion.
acceptanceCriteria: Library contains at least 15 architecture patterns with descriptions and trade-off analysis
Each pattern has a canvas-ready template that can be inserted with one click
Search and category filtering allows quick discovery of relevant patterns

title: Build Daily Design Challenge with AI generation and global leaderboard
description: Create a daily challenge feature where AI generates a unique system design prompt each day. All users solve the same challenge with a global leaderboard ranking solutions by score.
acceptanceCriteria: New challenge is generated daily at midnight UTC and presented identically to all users
Global leaderboard ranks all submissions by score with percentile display
Users can view top-scoring solutions after submitting their own (anti-cheating measure)

title: Build Multiplayer Design Battles with Elo ranking
description: Create a 1v1 competitive mode where two users solve the same challenge simultaneously. AI judges both solutions, awards the win, and updates Elo rankings.
acceptanceCriteria: Matchmaking pairs users of similar Elo rating within 60 seconds
Both players see a shared timer and submit simultaneously for AI judging
Elo rating updates after each match with K-factor adjustment for new players

title: Build Design Review Mode with peer review and inline annotations
description: Create a peer review mode where users can annotate another user's architecture with inline comments, suggestions, and ratings. Reviewers earn XP for providing helpful feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewers can click any node or edge to attach an inline annotation comment
Review includes an overall rating (1-5 stars) and structured feedback categories
Reviewers earn XP proportional to the helpfulness rating of their review

title: Build Achievement Trees with visual skill tree and unlockable progression
description: Create a visual skill tree where achievements are nodes connected by prerequisite paths. Users progress through branches (scalability, reliability, performance, etc.) unlocking deeper skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Skill tree renders as a navigable graph with branches for each domain area
Nodes show locked/unlocked state with prerequisite edges clearly visible
Unlocking a node triggers a visual unlock animation along the connecting path

title: Build Time Attack Mode with automated checks
description: Create a time-pressure challenge mode where users must design a system under strict time constraints. Automated checks validate the architecture progressively, with a final score based on completeness and time taken.
acceptanceCriteria: Time Attack mode starts with a compressed timer (50% of normal challenge time)
Automated checks run every 30 seconds and show a progressive completion percentage
Final score weights both architecture quality (70%) and speed bonus (30%)

title: Build Streak Protector Micro-Challenges
description: Create 2-minute focused micro-challenges that users can complete to save their streak on busy days. Tasks include quick quizzes, single-component placement, or concept recall exercises.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 10 micro-challenge types are defined, each completable in under 2 minutes
Completing a micro-challenge counts as daily activity for streak maintenance
Micro-challenges are contextually selected based on user's weakest areas

title: Build Concept Linker Knowledge Graph
description: Create an interactive knowledge graph that visualizes connections between system design concepts (e.g., caching links to consistency, latency, TTL). Users can navigate by clicking concept nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: Knowledge graph contains at least 50 concepts with labeled relationship edges
Clicking a concept node expands its connections and shows a brief explanation panel
Graph supports search/filter to quickly locate a specific concept

title: Build Protocol Deep-Dive edge interaction
description: Enable users to zoom into edges (connections) to see detailed protocol interaction visualizations: request/response flows, handshakes, data format, and throughput characteristics.
acceptanceCriteria: Double-clicking an edge opens a protocol deep-dive panel for that connection
Panel shows protocol type (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket, etc.) with request/response sequence diagram
At least 5 protocol types have detailed interaction visualizations

title: Build Architecture Evolution Timeline with snapshots
description: Create a timeline feature that auto-saves architecture snapshots at key moments and lets users browse the evolution of their design over time. Supports manual snapshot labels and diff between any two points.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshots are auto-saved on significant actions (add 5+ nodes, submit evaluation, etc.)
Timeline scrubber shows labeled snapshots that can be previewed on hover
Users can diff any two snapshots to see what changed between them

title: Build AI War Stories with production incident scenarios
description: Create an AI-powered feature that generates realistic production incident scenarios based on the user's architecture. Presents the incident as a narrative with progressive clues for the user to diagnose.
acceptanceCriteria: AI generates a realistic incident scenario based on the current architecture's weak points
Scenario is presented as a time-series narrative with escalating symptoms
User can propose root cause and fix, with AI validating their diagnosis

title: Build AI Difficulty Adaptation with Bayesian skill model
description: Implement a Bayesian skill model using Thompson sampling to adaptively select challenge difficulty. Model updates beliefs about user skill per topic based on performance history.
acceptanceCriteria: Bayesian model maintains per-topic skill estimates with uncertainty bounds
Thompson sampling selects difficulty that maximizes learning (targets ~70% success zone)
Model cold-starts reasonably for new users and converges within 10 challenge completions

title: Build AI Explanation Mode with Alt+hover contextual help
description: Create a contextual help mode activated by Alt+hover that uses AI to generate brief explanations of any component, connection, or pattern on the canvas. Explains what it is and why it is used.
acceptanceCriteria: Alt+hover on any node shows a tooltip with AI-generated explanation within 1 second
Explanations are contextual: describe the component's role within the current architecture
Explanation cache prevents redundant AI calls for the same component in the same session

title: Build Data Flow Sankey Diagram
description: Create a Sankey diagram visualization showing proportional data flow between components. Flow width represents data volume, enabling users to identify bandwidth-heavy paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Sankey diagram renders data flows with width proportional to configured throughput
Users can configure throughput values per edge to see flow proportions update
Diagram highlights the top 3 bandwidth-heavy paths with a summary legend

title: Build Geographic View with world map and data center locations
description: Create a world map view showing data center locations, CDN PoPs, and user population centers. Overlay latency lines between regions to visualize global architecture distribution.
acceptanceCriteria: SVG world map renders with clickable regions to place data centers
Latency lines between data centers show estimated round-trip time
User population overlay shows color-coded density for major geographic markets

title: Build 3D Architecture City View with Three.js
description: Create a 3D visualization mode using Three.js where system components are represented as buildings in a city. Building height represents load, color represents health, and streets represent connections.
acceptanceCriteria: Three.js scene renders components as 3D buildings with height based on configured load
Building color reflects health status (green=healthy, yellow=warning, red=critical)
Camera controls support orbit, zoom, and click-to-inspect individual buildings

title: Build Architecture Gallery with public repository
description: Create a public gallery where users can publish their architecture designs. Supports forking, starring, commenting, and browsing by category, popularity, and recency.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can publish a design to the gallery with title, description, and tags
Gallery supports browsing with sort by stars/recency and filter by category/difficulty
Fork creates a copy in the user's workspace; star and comment counts are tracked

title: Build Real-Time Study Groups with collaborative canvas and voice chat
description: Create real-time collaborative study rooms where multiple users share a canvas and communicate via voice chat. Uses WebSocket for real-time sync and WebRTC for voice.
acceptanceCriteria: Up to 4 users can join a study room and see each other's cursor and edits in real-time
Voice chat connects via WebRTC with mute/unmute and speaker indicators
Canvas state synchronizes within 100ms latency using WebSocket CRDT or OT

title: Build Mentor Mode with expert annotation layer and voice notes
description: Create a mentor mode where experts can record voice note annotations attached to specific canvas locations. Mentees can replay annotations in context while studying the architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: Mentors can record voice notes (up to 60 seconds) pinned to specific canvas nodes/regions
Mentees see annotation markers on the canvas and can click to play the voice note
Voice notes are stored efficiently with compression and streamed on playback

title: Build Weekly Community Challenge with voting and prizes
description: Create a weekly community challenge where all users solve the same design problem. Community votes on submissions, and top designs earn prizes (XP bonuses, special badges, featured status).
acceptanceCriteria: New community challenge launches every Monday with a 7-day submission window
Community voting runs for 48 hours after submission deadline with ranked-choice voting
Top 3 submissions earn tiered XP bonuses and a special weekly champion badge

title: Build Office Hours Live Sessions with presenter mode and recording
description: Create a live session feature where experts can present architecture designs with a presenter mode (spotlight, drawing tools, laser pointer). Sessions are recorded for async viewing.
acceptanceCriteria: Presenter mode spotlights the presenter's view with drawing tools and laser pointer overlay
Audience members can follow the presenter's view in real-time with a raise-hand queue
Sessions are recorded and available for replay within 5 minutes of ending

title: Build Intent-Aware Collaboration Cursors with ghost previews
description: Enhance collaboration cursors to show ghost previews of what other users are about to do. Before a node is placed, collaborators see a faded ghost preview at the target position.
acceptanceCriteria: Collaborator cursors show a ghost preview of the component being dragged before placement
Ghost previews use 30% opacity and the correct node type icon
Previews disappear immediately when the action is completed or cancelled

title: Build Sound Design System with Web Audio feedback
description: Create a subtle sound design system using Web Audio API for interaction feedback: soft clicks on node placement, whooshes on connections, chimes on achievements, and ambient workspace sounds.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 8 distinct sound effects mapped to actions (place, connect, delete, achieve, level-up, etc.)
Sounds are subtle and professional with volume control and global mute toggle
Web Audio API generates sounds procedurally to minimize asset download size

title: Build Multi-Region Visualization with SVG world map and latency lines
description: Create a multi-region architecture view using an SVG world map overlay. Show data center placements per region with latency lines indicating cross-region communication costs.
acceptanceCriteria: SVG world map correctly renders major cloud regions (US-East, EU-West, AP-Southeast, etc.)
Data centers placed on the map show latency lines with millisecond labels to connected regions
Map integrates with the main canvas so region-level components can be expanded to full architecture

title: Build Plugin Architecture with iframe sandbox and permissions
description: Create a plugin system that allows third-party extensions via sandboxed iframes. Plugins register capabilities, request permissions, and communicate with the host app through a secure message API.
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin manifest schema defines name, version, permissions (canvas-read, canvas-write, ai-access), and entry URL
Plugins run in sandboxed iframes with postMessage communication to the host
Permission system prompts user to approve requested permissions on first install
